title: pilea radicans
intro: Pilea radicans, also known as Creeping Wire Vine or Creeping Charlie, is a perennial plant species in the family Urticaceae. It is a climbing plant that is native to Southeast Asia and is often used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is said to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating digestive issues. In herbal preparations, Pilea radicans is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
